objective: Write a specific user persona and identify the Job To Be Done behind a real Indian app complaint.

realWorldAnchor.example: CRED's decision to only allow users with credit scores above 750 — a deliberate persona-driven product choice that defined their entire brand

tasks.title: The Jobs To Be Done Framework
tasks.summary: Jobs To Be Done (JTBD) is a framework for understanding why customers buy or use products. Instead of focusing on demographics, it asks: what 'job' is the user hiring this product to do? This lens leads to far better product decisions than persona-only thinking.
keyTakeaways: Users don't buy products — they hire them to do a job in their life
The same product can be hired for very different jobs by different users
Identifying the core job unlocks better positioning, features, and messaging
JTBD pairs powerfully with personas to create a complete user picture

tasks.title: How to Write User Personas That Actually Work
tasks.summary: Most personas are useless because they're full of demographic fluff. This article explains how to write actionable personas grounded in real user behaviour, motivations, and frustrations — the kind that actually inform product decisions.
keyTakeaways: A persona must include a specific frustration, not just an age and job title
Good personas come from user interviews, not assumptions
One strong persona beats five weak ones
Always anchor personas to a real product decision you need to make

tasks.keyword: JTBD framework product manager real examples India
tasks.whyItMatters: Most JTBD examples online are American SaaS products. You need to find Indian consumer app examples to build the muscle of applying this framework in your actual market context.
whatToLearn: How Indian PMs talk about user jobs in blog posts or Twitter threads
Any teardowns of Indian super-apps using JTBD thinking
How functional vs emotional vs social jobs differ in Indian consumer behaviour
Examples where an Indian app changed based on understanding user jobs

tasks.title: Identify the Job Being Hired
tasks.claudePrompt:
You are a PM coach running a live coaching session with an APM candidate.

Today's topic is Jobs To Be Done (JTBD).

Start by asking the learner to pick ONE Indian app he uses daily and describe ONE specific moment when he used it.

Then guide him through these questions one at a time:
1. 'What were you doing or feeling just before you opened this app?'
2. 'What did you actually want to accomplish — not the feature, but the underlying goal?'
3. 'Is that goal functional, emotional, or social?'
4. 'Could you have hired a different product to do this same job?'
5. 'Now write a JTBD statement: When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome]'

After his JTBD statement, give specific feedback on specificity, motivation depth, and outcome measurability.

Score 1-10 with one clear improvement suggestion.

bonusTasks.title: Why Dunzo Failed — A JTBD Analysis
bonusTasks.summary: Dunzo built a beloved product but failed to stay aligned with the job users were hiring it for. As Zepto and Blinkit redefined quick commerce, Dunzo lost its JTBD focus.
keyTakeaways: Being first to a job doesn't mean you keep it
Expanding into too many jobs dilutes your core value
Unit economics matter more when users have identical alternatives

assessmentTask.taskDescription: Persona + JTBD exercise using real Zepto complaints
assessmentTask.rawMaterial:
Here are 5 real-style Zepto app store reviews:

Review 1 (⭐⭐): "Ordered 4 items. They delivered 3 and said the 4th was out of stock — AFTER I already paid. Why show it in stock at all?"

Review 2 (⭐): "The delivery guy called me 6 times in 2 minutes while I was in a meeting. There should be a 'do not call' option."

Review 3 (⭐⭐⭐): "Good for quick top-ups but pricing is way higher than a kirana store. I only use it when I'm desperate."

Review 4 (⭐⭐): "Why is the app so pushy about Zepto Cafe? I came to buy vegetables. Stop cross-selling."

Review 5 (⭐⭐⭐⭐): "Use it mostly when I forget something while cooking. Faster than stepping out. But please fix the search — typing 'atta' shows branded options before loose flour."
assessmentTask.outputFormat:
1. ONE specific user persona (name, age, living situation, one core frustration, one goal)
2. ONE JTBD statement: 'When [situation], I want to [motivation], so I can [outcome]'
3. ONE feature recommendation with a 1-sentence rationale
assessmentTask.claudePrompt:
You are a senior PM reviewing the Day 3 assessment of APM candidate the learner.

He was given 5 Zepto reviews and asked to write a persona, JTBD statement, and feature recommendation.

**What I Got Right**
**What Needs Correction**
**Blind Spots**
**India-Specific Note**
**Open Points for Tomorrow**

Score: X/10
Reforge Level: Needs Focus / On Track / Outperform
